C: Sample Plan Adherence (SPA) | D: It is the sum of (minimum of either the samples delivered or the planned(apportioned) samples for each physician) in the selected geography/ the planned(apportioned) samples.

dcv_metric_definition.xlsx.text:
Columns: Metric Definition

C: Adherence | D: 1) Depending on how many times a target was called when compared to planned PDE, adherence is decided

2) If a target is called 80-120% of the planned PDE, it is optimally called. Above it is over called and below it is under called

3) If the total no. of PDE is less than 10, there are seperate product specific rules | E: Geography, product, Segment, Specialty | F: QTD | G: x=Sum of PDE to targets/Planned PDE

x>=0.8 or x<=1.2 => Optimally Called

x<0.8 => Under Called

x>1.2 => Over Called

Metric

Definitions

QTD Volume | Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Territory Level
Nat. QTD Vol. | Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Nation Level
LTD Volume | Units sold in Launch Till Date time period at Territory Level
Nat. LTD Vol. | Units sold in Launch Till Date time period at Nation Level
QTD Sales Attainment | Ratio of Units sold in QTD time period to its respective QTD goal at Territory Level
Nat. Sales Attn. | Ratio of Units sold in QTD time period to its respective QTD goal at Nation Level
Growth (13) | % Growth calculated at rolling 13 weeks time period
Previous QTR Volume | Units sold in Previous Quarter at Territory Level
Growth (vs Prev QTR) | % Growth calculated at QTD Time Period
Vol Change (vs Prev QTR) | Volume Change calculated at QTD Time Period

C13W % Patients PSP Util. | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment in current 13 weeks
P13W % Patients PSP Util. | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment in previous 13 weeks
13 Wks. % Pat. PSP Utilized | Difference in current and previous % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollments

QTD <Segment> Reach | % of Target reach for the specific <Segment> in QTD Time Period
QTD <Segment> Freq. | Ratio of Total number of interactions to the total number of target customers for the specific <Segment>

Dupi AD NBRx from Claims Data (R12M) | New Dupixent patients (AD NBRx) are those who have either

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: Scope
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Scope

The Scope of this document included the Phase-I Qlik Sense implementation of the Medical report.

The dashboard shows the lifecycle of an interaction.

How the medical service liaison reaches out to a HCP/HCA, how the information is shared in terms of an Interaction or a Presentation, the details being discussed, the mindset of the HCP/HCAs and getting the information and tracking the journey of the various inquiries (MIRs) sent by the HCP/HCAs for a grievance.

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Columns: Rule, Rule/Description
Row 1: Time, Starting from July 2017 to the latest week’s data
Row 2: Time, Both Medical HCA and HCP reports are refreshed on a weekly basis
Row 3: User Security, Section Access based on geographical assignment of medical team
Row 4: Sales Teams - HCP, 
Row 5: Sales Teams - HCA, 
Row 6: Business Units, Diabetes, CV, Biosurgery, Oncology, Endo, MS, Rare Disease, Genetic Disease etc.
Row 7: Customer Universe, All Affiliated Physicians and Accounts – Current Profiles
Row 8: Calls, No Call Only/Sample Only calls
Call Status should be in Submitted Only
Row 9: KOL/NKOL, Engage the product metrics with customer profile table to drive these values, if a customer has a flag 1, then it would be Key opinion leader, otherwise not

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation
Row 1: # Interactions (2-Way), # of Unique HCP Interactions = #Interactions KOL + #Interactions Non-KOL
# of Unique HCA Interactions = #Interactions Non-KOL
All Activity corresponding to following Record Type Names:
Event_vod, Interactions, Medical Non-Verified
MSL Meeting Brief_vod, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions","Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 2: # Interactions KOL, No of Interactions to KOL Accounts, Count(Distinct Call ID) where KOL Flag=1 and Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions", "Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 3: # Interactions Non-KOL, No of Interactions to Non-KOL Accounts, Count(Distinct Call ID) where KOL Flag=0 and Record Type Name in ("Event_vod",
"Interactions",
"Medical Non Verified",
"MSL Meeting Brief_vod")
Row 4: # Interaction prod Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL had a 2-way exchange with X the total products/disease states discussed, (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Interaction Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL) x products/disease states discussed
Row 5: # Interaction Touchpoints KOL, All unique Interaction engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, # Interactions with KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 6: # Interaction Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique Interaction engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, # Interactions with Non-KOL x # Prod Discussed

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: KPIs
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Columns: KPIs, Business Definition, Calculation
Row 7: # Presentation (1-Way), Unique Presentation Meetings (i.e. Only parent meeting)
All Activity corresponding to:
Record Type Name: "Presentation", Count(Distinct Parent Call ID) where KOL Flag=1 and Record Type Name="Presentation"
Row 8: # Presentation Attendees, Number of attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value), Sum(# Presentation Attendees)
Row 9: # Presentation Attendees KOL, #Presentation Attendee as KOL, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name="Presentation" and KOL Flag=1
Row 10: # Presentation Attendees Non-KOL, #Presentation Attendee as Non-KOL, Count(Distinct Call ID) where Record Type Name="Presentation" and KOL Flag=0
Row 11: # Presentation Attendees Others, # of Unique Presentation Instances corresponding to Non-Profiled attendees, (# Presentation Attendees) - (# Presentation Attendees KOL + # Presentation Attendees Non-KOL)
#Presentation Attendee (Non-Profiled)
Row 12: # Presentation Prod Touchpoints, Total number of people the MSL had a 1-way exchange with X the total products/disease states discussed, Total Number of Presentation Attendees entered in Veeva Medical (number value) x # Prod Discussed
Row 13: # Presentation Touchpoints KOL, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, # Presentation with KOL x # Prod Discussed
Row 14: # Presentation Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique Presentation engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, # Presentation with Non-KOL x # Prod Discussed

Row 21: # Product Touchpoints KOL, All unique engagements corresponding to KOL accounts and products, Total Interact/Present KOL (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints KOL + #Presentation Prod Touchpoints KOL) x # Prod Discussed
Row 22: # Product Touchpoints Non-KOL, All unique engagements corresponding to Non-KOL accounts and products, Total Interact/Present Non- KOL (#Interaction Prod Touchpoints Non-KOL + #Presentation Prod Touchpoints Non- KOL) x # Prod Discussed

Medical Report_Requirement Specification.heading: Purpose of the Document
Medical Report_Requirement Specification.text:
Purpose of the Document

This document captures detailed user requirements to build a tool to enhance Business Analytics capabilities of Sanofi team to analyze the Medical Reporting Ecosystem, by leveraging the centrally developed and supported CIM system and building an Ad-hoc analytics layer on top of it.

This new Ad-hoc Analytics layer would be based on Qlik Sense platform and would be primarily developed for Sanofi team to carry out different analysis including field leadership, Regional Medical Liaisons, HQ staff and senior executives.

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.heading: Enterprise Realities That Shaped Platform Evolution
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Enterprise Realities That Shaped Platform Evolution

[image]

[image]

[image]

[image]

Minimize Impact Across Ecosystem

Business continuity across a complex downstream ecosystem was prioritized over structural redesign.

Launch Timelines & Legacy Sunset Pressure

Aggressive launch timelines and committed legacy decommissioning dates constrained opportunities for redesign

TA Evolution and regression considerations

Therapeutic-area needs evolved faster than enterprise alignment mechanisms.

Governance around Architecture Guidelines

Delivery governance emphasized outcomes and speed over continuous architectural recalibration.

Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities

2022–23:

Urgent

Build

2023–24:

Stabilize

&

Expand

2024–25:

Scale

&

Enable

2026:

Modernized

Enterprise

Business Trigger

Legacy system (CIM/SGDW) unable to process Dupixent data (constant issues and failures)

Requirement for diamond platform to onboard Dupixent processes and share process outbounds to CIM/SGDW in legacy format

No formal discovery phase to identify cross brand requirements

Business Context

Integration of different data domains like sales, patient, CRM etc. for 3 more TAs - RBD, Oncology, Kevzara

Increasing downstream dependencies led to lower appetite for change management and overall Retrofitment

Leadership changes (e.g., departure of Susan) altered decision authority

Business Context

Multi-brand onboarding: Dupixent, Rare Disease, Tzield, MS Tolebrutinib on the existing platform

Launch readiness requirements

Business Context

Integration of GenMeds, IC, FD

Mandate to sunset CIM/SGDW

Architectural Trade-Off

Diamond positioned as landing & transformation layer for all the onboarding brands

Maintained downstream data formats to align with data management requirements

Data contracts not enforced enterprise-wide

Key Inflection

Operational continuity prioritized over data rationalization

Decommissioning of legacy system post go live of each brand

Strategic Outcome

Diamond becomes commercial data warehouse for all Brands/Products

Complete retirement of Legacy CIM Processing (Q3’26)

Architectural Decision

Designed for rapid onboarding to cater Dupixent specific business needs

Narrow scope aligned to a single brand requirement not for entire Commercial portfolio

Key Inflection

Architecture shaped for a specific product, not enterprise modeling (brand agnostic)

Architectural Pattern

Standardized onboarding pathway via Diamond

Continued need of retrofitting due to evolving brand needs

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.heading: Issues and Reasons
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Theme | Description | Impact
Speed vs. Sustainability | The platform was developed under tight timelines (≤ 1.5 years) with no formal discovery phase.
The urgent onboarding of Dupixent took priority over enterprise re-architecture.
Design decisions were optimized for rapid deployment, not long-term scalability or standardization. | The system met immediate business needs but accumulated architectural constraints that limit flexibility and modernization.
Brand Urgency vs. Enterprise Standardization | The architecture was shaped primarily by the needs of Dupixent, rather than a holistic enterprise model.
Dupixent could not be processed in the legacy system, requiring a fast-track solution.
Coding and logic were often tailored by Therapeutic Areas (TAs)
Enterprise-wide data modeling (sum of all needs approach) was not completed. | The platform became brand-driven rather than standardized, creating fragmentation across Therapeutic Areas (TAs).
Operational Continuity vs. Data Rationalization | Maintaining business continuity outweighed structural data cleanup.
Downstream systems required data in the same format.
Full data rationalization would have broken existing integrations.
Data contracts were not enforced across the board.
The sunsetting of CIM/SGDW/DSS accelerated migration without redesign time. | Diamond operated primarily as a landing and transformation layer, rather than a fully rationalized enterprise warehouse.
Directive Decisions vs. Architectural Vision | Governance dynamics influenced architectural outcomes.
Some decisions were directive rather than architecturally driven.
Proposed improvements were highlighted but not fully executed.
Leadership changes (e.g., departure of Susan) altered decision authority.
High change management overhead discouraged re-architecture. | Strategic architectural evolution slowed, reinforcing tactical delivery choices.

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.heading: Issues and Reasons
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
S. No. | Issue | Description
1 | Source Data Rationalization | TA wise Roadmap to enable diamond, leading to lack of clarity of all expected future data sources
Priority to enable Dupixent Foundation in diamond, focusing on Dupixent design rather than designing the Commercial Warehouse
Requirements to use same source data as legacy reducing the opportunity to rationalize feeds from different products
Diamond was a base system and source for multiple parallel systems like Turing, Sapphire. Thus, diamond layouts were in sync with the requirements for parallel systems as well
2 | Downstream Specific redundant datasets | Due to TA-by-TA Integration, Any changes to existing production deliverables led to additional change management overhead
Timeline Crunch, due to pre aligned legacy decommissioning timelines focus was on timely delivery instead of robust and scalable design
3 | Data Architecture | Data Layers initial aligned with the Sanofi DE Team, was reviewed and aligned but due to committed timelines the design was never looked back or retrofitted to improve

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.text:
Area | Deliverables | Owners
Requirements
Gathering | Document business requirements for Dupixent PSS solutions and Downstream consumers
Coordination and synthesis of requirements for other non-Dupixent PSS solutions | PSS A&R (ZS Team) & ICI Team
| Non-Dupixent business requirements and other ad-hoc Requirements in the provided BRD template | PSS A&R (Sanofi Team)
Requirements
Mapping | Mapping business requirements to data requirements to CADs and RRDs | PSS A&R (ZS Team) & ICI Team
| Sign-off on Mapping documents | PSS A&R (Sanofi Team)
Design and Data
Modelling | High-level architecture, Logical and physical data model and ER diagram for the CADs and RRDs | PSS A&R (ZS Team) & ICI Team
| Source to Target Mapping any other standard supporting document | PSS A&R (ZS Team) & ICI Team
Implementation
& Testing | Validate CADs, RRDs against Logical Data Model
Develop Metadata (attributes, description, ontologies and synonyms/tags) | PSS A&R (ZS Team) & ICI Team
| Fully developed and tested CADs and RRDs | PSS A&R (ZS Team) & ICI Team
| Finalized playbooks for Incident Management, Service Request handling, and Change Management processes. | PSS A&R (ZS Team)
| Finalized governance playbook defining decision rights, controls, and standards across DevOps process | PSS A&R (ZS Team)
Validation | Provide UAT support | PSS A&R (Sanofi, ZS Team) & ICI Team

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.heading: Project Assumptions (1/2)
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.text:
Project Assumptions (1/2)

Requirements & Inputs Assumptions

PSS A&R and ICI team to provide complete, accurate, and timely business requirements for non-Dupixent TAs and downstream consumers using the ZS Business and Data requirements template

ZS will receive all necessary contextual information on use cases, KBQs, KPIs, and downstream reporting needs

Any changes or additions to requirements may impact timelines and effort post the requirements phase closure

Requirements from PSS A&R and ICI will be signed off before build begins

Dependencies on upstream systems (e.g., source data owners) will be managed by Sanofi teams

Data Availability & Quality Assumptions

Required source data for finalized CADs and RRDs will be available and accessible to Design and Development team

Data issues (missing fields, unexpected values, quality problems) will be resolved by Digital/Source System owners

All the relevant infrastructure, systems/database accesses, schema-level accesses will be provided by Sanofi Digital to support the project

Data Validations will depend on representative sample outputs being provided by Digital

Design & Build Assumptions

ZS Team will be onboarded on to the existing ICI Data Model and PADs and design of up to 2 CADs and 10 RRDs are assumed as part of scope

ZS and ICI to ensure that the proposed Data Products will be scalable across TAs and markets

Design inputs (BRD, KPI mapping, metadata, etc.) will be treated as source of truth during implementation

Any architectural constraints or sign-offs (OneMesh, iCare Insights ecosystem) will be clarified with Digital team

Platform (iCare Insights, Snowflake, PBI ecosystem, etc.) is stable, accessible, and managed by Digital including DevOps

Project Assumptions (1/2)

Platform (iCare Insights, Snowflake, PBI ecosystem, etc.) is stable, accessible, and managed by Digital including DevOps

The metadata creation for the CADs and RRDs will include – table and attribute names and detailed descriptions, along with ontologies/tags in English for 2 CADs and 10 RRDs

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx::slide_11::shape_4::text

PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.chunk_id: PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx::chunk_0025
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.source_file: PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.heading: Project Assumptions (2/2)
PSS A&R Data Products Proposal v1.pptx.text:
Project Assumptions (2/2)

Downstream Retrofitment Assumptions

PBI dashboard retrofitment to be performed by Sanofi Hub team, timeline assumed is 8-9 weeks for the same

ZS provides guidance, SOPs, and oversight and other guidance on hands-on retrofitment for initial 6 weeks

Validation & Testing Assumptions

The estimates are based on the assumption that ZS and ICI will be co-responsible for designing the CADs, STTMs, development of code, unit testing, and end to end testing of scoped CADs/RRDs

All defects must be logged, triaged and resolved jointly

UAT coordination, environment readiness, and user access are owned by Sanofi Digital

UAT sign-off timelines depend on stakeholder availability from PSS A&R

Governance & Collaboration Assumptions

Cross-team governance meetings will be scheduled consistently (weekly/bi-weekly sessions + milestone reviews).

Decision logs and risk logs maintained by ZS will be accepted as formal artifacts.

Timeline & Estimation Assumptions

Major requirement changes, new KPIs, new data sources, or new TAs may require timeline and cost adjustments.

Parallel analytics workstreams will continue independently without impacting this program

The following terms and conditions apply solely to work performed under this IPA and do not apply to any other IPA.

The IPA is governed by the terms of the Master Service Agreement dated February 22, 2021, as amended between Sanofi US Services Inc.

and ZS Associates, Inc.

(“MSA”).

To the extent there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this IPA and the MSA, the MSA controls.

All capitalized terms in this IPA will have the same defined meanings as set forth in the MSA.

This IPA shall be effective upon acceptance of the purchase order.

PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.heading: Project Summary:
PSS AR KPI Enablement through ICI Snowfl.text:
Project Summary:

Sanofi’s

PSS

A&R

team

is

looking

to

evolve

its

analytics

ecosystem

to

align

with

Digital’s

OneMesh

/

Data

Mesh

principles,

enabling

scalable,

reusable,

and

AI-ready

data

assets.

The objective of this engagement is to design and develop Consumption-Aligned Data Assets (CADs) and Reporting Ready Datasets (RRDs).

These data assets will be aligned with Digital’s OneMesh data principles and are AI-ready for future “Talk to Data” capabilities to enable OnePSS dashboards with built-in KPIs, metadata, and configurable business rules.

We will be leveraging existing Process-Aligned Data Assets (PADs) owned and managed by Sanofi ICI team

ZS

will

partner

with

Sanofi’s

Digital

ICI

and

PSS

A&R

teams

to

collaboratively

design,

implement,

validate,

and

transition

CADs/RRDs

supporting

current

and

future

analytics

use

cases

across

therapy

areas.

The

project

will

commence

on

Feb

9th,

2026

and

will

be

completed

on

June

26th,

2026

Columns: Key Activities, Details, Expected Outcomes/Deliverables
Row 1: Onboarding and Requirements Gathering, Conduct formal kickoff sessions with Sanofi stakeholders to align objectives, scope, governance, and ways of working
Establish communication plan, governance cadence (steering, working sessions), and initial project plan
Provision access for the project team to required systems and tools (e.g., Snowflake, Informatica, Confluence, SharePoint)
Finalize team structure, RACI, and onboarding plan for all stakeholders
Structured onboarding to Sanofi ecosystem and review of existing ICI PAD data models, architecture, DQ framework and design rationale
Gather requirements into a business requirement document template and validate business requirements from PSS A&R stakeholders, Onboarding: domain access, infrastructure and other tools and tech accesses set up for each team member.
Established program governance cadence and operating model
Duly filled and approved business requirements document for PSS A&R use cases (Dupixent)
JIRA user stories
Row 2: Requirements Mapping, Map approved PSS A&R business requirements to available source systems and existing ICI-owned PADs, including identification of data grain and KPI calculation logic.
Validation and sign-off of Dupixent PSS A&R mappings by Sanofi stakeholders prior to design initiation., Requirements-to-data mapping document (Dupixent, Other TAs and Adhoc use cases)
Signed-off mapping documentation
Row 3: Design, Work with Sanofi Architects/ICI team on designing requirements to Data Assets mapping
Finalization and preparation of DQ checks
Preparation of High-level and low-level design for data objects including
Data Models
Source to Target Mapping
Metadata tagging, Data Assets Archetypes (Requirements to new Data Assets mapping)

Metadata addition
Addition of DQ checks
End to end automation and orchestration configurations, Data Pipelines and code repositories

Row 5: SIT, Creation of Test Strategy, Test scripts
Preparation / Execution of test cases considering positive and negative data scenarios
End to end testing of all modules
Document SIT results, defects, and co-ordinate with development team for defect resolution.

Test Case scripts

Prepare UAT Test Cases/Scripts.
Collaborate with Sanofi BOS users and support them with executing & performing UAT validations.

Creation of required cutover and knowledge transfer documentation and KT workshops
Provide documentation, oversight, SOPs, and advisory support for 6 weeks to enable downstream Power BI dashboard retrofitment by Sanofi PSS A&R HUB teams, Hypercare support and handover artifacts (SOP documents)
Operations, enhancement, and KPI governance documentation

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Vendor Project Contact: Gagan Bhatara

The following terms and conditions apply solely to work performed under this IPA and do not apply to any other IPA.

The IPA is governed by the terms of the MSA dated February 22, 2021 between and ZS Associates, Inc.

(“MSA”).

To the extent there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this IPA and the MSA, the MSA controls.

All capitalized terms in this IPA will have the same defined meanings as set forth in the MSA.

Exit Clause: With respect to the Services outlined in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience with immediate effect.

In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to termination.

In Scope

The

of

OneCI

in-scope:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0014
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: In Scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Columns: Category, Activities
Row 1: Medical, Conduct requirements gathering sessions(1-2) with LUCI and OneCI Team
Design 2 new medical views, along with adding their source views to the OneCRM Foundation Data Mart
Update the current IICS mappings across 3 layers - staging, DWH and Data Mart for OneCRM
Create URS/FRS and STTM, Data models document to incorporate OneCI above country medical changes.
Updates in the existing approved URS/FRS documents for OneCRM and OneCI
Co-ordination with OneCI Team, LUCI Team, One CRM
Row 2: Data Quality checks, Design & build for business data quality checks, according to the requirements provided by LUCI
URS/FRS updates to reflect the DQ requirements, TDD updates
Creation of DQ queries for required scenarios, integration of DQ module
End to end testing and sign-off

Any event that occurs during the project, which will require alteration of the project plan or induces a risk to quality and schedule of the project, will be deemed as a change.

Activities that need design, code or testing changes that are not included as part of current scope, will be considered as enhancements.

If the requested changes/enhancements are beyond the original scope of the project, and/or exceed the overall timeline of the project, a separate change request will be raised with additional cost to extend the project scope/timeline.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Roles & Responsibilities

ZS

understands

that

the

success

of

this

project

is

driven

by

right

mix

of

people

with

technology

and

domain

expertise.

Following

is

a

description

of

high-level

roles

and

responsibilities

of

key

ZS

and

Sanofi

Resources:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0029
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Roles & Responsibilities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Onshore ZS Team, Onshore ZS Team
Row 2: Senior Business Analyst/Integration Lead, Has good domain knowledge around Sanofi data and business applications.
Engage and communicate with Sanofi stakeholders as required during product requirements definition, design, and development.
Coordination with onshore team on data findings and data analysis results
Row 3: Offshore ZS Team, Offshore ZS Team
Row 4: Technical Team Lead, Responsible for day-to-day management of project
Responsible to maintaining timelines and quality of all deliverables.
Provide oversight of technical solution design and implementation
Ensure adherence to Sanofi program management framework and standards in delivery.
Row 5: Sr. Developer, Review requirements and design and raise clarifying questions before starting build.
Review design and develop POCs along with the design team.
Review and contribute to the design with the overall solution in mind.
Lead and perform code reviews, with the understanding of overall solution and client coding standards.
Lead with the Design/build activities.
Manage day to day build activities and coordinate with offshore delivery lead to ensure progress is being made as per plan.
Row 6: Test Analyst, Prepare test cases based on scenarios defined
For an issues / concerns around design, requirement and scenarios – raise the same in defect tracker and discuss the same on daily calls 
Create test status report tool and share daily status report using the tool
During UAT phase, retest UAT defects in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Sanofi roles & responsibilities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Program Manager, Act at the liaison between ZS and Sanofi leadership
Row 2: PMO, Work in tandem with ZS Project/Testing Coordinator to collaborate with various Sanofi core team/downstream/Infrastructure teams
Provide status updates to Sanofi core team
Row 3: System Owner, Overall business requirements/demand owner
Help with DSS / SGDW retirement plan from a Sanofi standpoint
Row 4: User Project Leader (UPL), Define & Approve user requirements (LUCI and OneCI team)
Provide user requirements for processes where documentation is not available
Provide inputs to come up with effective system retirement and data migration plan
Row 5: Technical Architect, Review & Approve design documents and architecture
Row 6: IS Project Leader (IPL), Review & Approve design documents and architecture
Approve code migration and deployments
Review & Approve test strategy/test cases/test results
Row 7: Business Quality Representative, Provide business inputs to formulate test strategy

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx::chunk_0033

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country Medical & OneCI DQ_09-OCT-2024.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.heading: Risk Management
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - Above Country.text:
Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach
Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown
requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with OneCI team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control
Row 2: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs., Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions.
Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues.
Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control
Row 3: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control
Row 4: OneCI/LUCI team’s availability, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner

The total maximum amount payable under this IPA will not exceed $ .

The not-to-exceed value quoted above is an estimate of the cost of the anticipated Services to be rendered and is not a guaranteed fee.

The total compensation paid to Vendor will be based on actual Services provided in accordance with the MSA.

The project plan and pricing set forth in this Sanofi project diamond OneCI enhancements do not take into consideration any price escalations, delays, shortages, and other disruptions that may occur due to the global COVID-19 (also known as coronavirus) pandemic and actions taken in response to the pandemic by businesses, local, state, and national authorities, or by ZS in its reasonable discretion (collectively, a “COVID-19 Event).

Sanofi acknowledges and agrees that ZS is permitted to adjust the timelines, fees and/or personnel assignments set forth in this SOW if, in its reasonable judgement, ZS determines that a Covid-19 Event will prevent it from delivering the project as set forth in this SOW.

ZS will provide Sanofi with prompt written notice of any such determination and, upon Sanofi’s request, will execute a change order to this SOW documenting any such changes.

The following terms and conditions apply solely to work performed under this IPA and do not apply to any other IPA.

The IPA is governed by the terms of the MSA dated February 22, 2021 between and ZS Associates, Inc.

(“MSA”).

To the extent there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this IPA and the MSA, the MSA controls.

All capitalized terms in this IPA will have the same defined meanings as set forth in the MSA.

Exit Clause: With respect to the Services outlined in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience with immediate effect.

In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to termination.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Project Summary
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Project Summary

The scope of Project Diamond is to transforms the on-premise data warehouses DSS (Decision Support System) and SGDW (Sanofi Genzyme Datawarehouse) and build a common Cloud based Datawarehouse platform for its Genzyme Specialty and General Medicine divisions to support U.S commercial business applications.

Through this initiative, Sanofi will develop an enterprise data management platform that will improve the advanced analytics capabilities and will prove to be a competitive advantage for Sanofi over its competitors.

DSS & SGDW currently run on on-prem Informatica Power Center and Oracle and have grown increasingly complex and the evolution in the business needs is nearly continuous.

Building the next generation data warehouse application on the cloud creates enormous opportunity to define a new model for innovation, operation and transformation to get ahead of the pace of the business.

As

part

of

this

initiative,

ZS

will

design,

build

and

deploy

all

processes,

that

exist

in

current

DSS

and

SGDW

platforms,

to

the

new

cloud-based

data

warehouse

platform

based

on

AWS

/

Informatica

Cloud

(IICS)

/

Snowflake.

In

order

to

gain

quicker

benefits

of

the

new

platform

and

mitigate

program

execution

risk,

this

project

approaches

the

onboarding

of

business

units

in

in

an

agile

manner

through

multiple

releases

and

ZS

will

follow

Sanofi’s

Agile

delivery

methodology

using

POD

structure

and

use

tools

like

JIRA

and

Confluence.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Project Diamond aims at providing below benefits –

Scalability: Build configurable frameworks and loosely coupled modules that allow faster onboarding of new datasets and scalable design for new enhancements.

The design will consider future re-usability and scalability of common components to other countries

Optimization

of

data

process:

Opportunity

to

improve

current

data

process

and

underlying

business

process

as

identified

by

business

stakeholders

in

requirements

and

design

session

aligned

to

the

global

data

governance

framework.

Accelerated

Performance:

Accelerate

processes

within

the

system

through

point

to

point

integration

across

applications

and

effective

utilization

of

the

cloud

infrastructure

to

process

sales

data

faster

by

25-30%

or

more

Data

Quality:

Configurable

data

quality

engine

with

business

DQ

checks

for

errors

that

commonly

affect

downstream

business

applications

that

will

significantly

improve

quality

of

data

across

the

platform

and

delivered

to

downstream

business

applications

Eliminate limitations due to data volume: Current legacy platform limits ability to ingest complex and large volume datasets like APLD etc.

These limitations will be eliminated by moving to cloud and optimizing / redesigning current data integration processes

The

overall

timeline

of

this

project

is

14

months

–

From

Nov

29,2021

to

Jan

31,

2023

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Downstream Applications Testing:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: S. No, User Group, Change Management Activities
Row 1: 1, Downstream Commercial Operations Business Applications, Downstream commercial applications like IC, FD, FR, BI, Veeva are major consumers of data from DSS / SGDW. Below are the high-level change management activities for these applications:
Data comparison and Training materials:
Perform data comparison between new and legacy datasets and highlight the discrepancies, if any
Prepare training materials to educate the downstream applications to start consuming data from the new cloud platform
Cut-over Strategy: 
As downstream business applications are dependent on Frozen Quarterly process, change management and a cut-over strategy will be planned to align with the frozen timelines and to minimize the impact 
Communication Plan
Establish the communication plan for downstream applications for awareness regarding system cut-off from legacy to new system
Discuss and plan the parallel run, cutover to new system.
Row 2: 2, Upstream Data Sources / Vendor Teams, Upstream sources integrate with DSS / SGDW for feeding data such as facts and dimensions. The major impact on such systems will be on the interface of integration. 
Interface Changes
Work with the respective upstream application IT teams to coordinate the change in delivery mechanism and path
Data Sources may have to feed to both legacy DSS / SGDW and Project Diamond for certain period and that will be planned and coordinated with the respective data vendor teams

Infrastructure

including

including

and

administration

by

/

GIS

out

of

for

ZS

Sanofi Project Diamond Team with guidance from digital data architecture team/tech team who will be responsible for setup / migration of environments from Dev to Test to Production.

ZS will coordinate with Sanofi digital data team on scheduling or performing the deployment activities per Sanofi guidance or formal processes.

As part of data catalog support, ZS will create and share initial data catalog metadata/glossaries.

Setup, development and deployment of Informatica Data Catalog is out of scope and to be managed by Sanofi Data Catalog team.

Ongoing updates to the metadata/glossaries will be managed by US Data Management team

With the new cloud platform, all upstream data sources will land their data into a Sanofi provisioned SFTP site or AWS S3 bucket, if direct connectivity is not available.

If direct connectivity is feasible, it will be used to establish ingestion.

The direct connectivity includes Snowflake Data Sharing mechanisms or accessing data from AWS S3.

Building a new API setup with any application/vendor is considered out of scope for the SOW

new

Sanofi

and

components

or

a

ZS will provide necessary data and support to carry out such activities by the respective applications.

/

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Project Delivery

Sanofi

Digital

/

and

and

and

Project

Diamond

implementation

to

or

in

existing

It is assumed that current DSS / SGDW business rules and data layouts are readily available to facilitate an efficient requirement gathering phase.

For processes where there is no documentation available, ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to document the process during requirements phase

Currently, all inbound data sources into DSS and outbound data sources to platforms like Veeva, RCM are sent via Integration Hub.

As Integration Hub is not part of the architecture for Project Diamond, it is assumed that all exchanges that were done via Integration Hub will be done via SFTP or AWS S3.

Any data transformations currently done in Integration Hub prior to sending data to DSS are assumed to be managed by the new replacement platform for Integration Hub.

If any of these assumptions change, the impact will be analyzed first and corresponding changes will be managed via change management process.

Project Diamond will plan to integrate with the new US Commercial Product MDM.

For timely integration, the overall timelines of new US Product MDM should sync up with Project Diamond releases and should be confirmed during Project Diamond Requirements/Design Phase.

It is assumed that data models / layouts and interfacing mechanism for new US Product MDM into Project Diamond will be provided during Project Diamond Requirements/Design phase.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Project Delivery

It is assumed that data models / layouts and interfacing mechanism for new US Product MDM into Project Diamond will be provided during Project Diamond Requirements/Design phase.

Any challenges with the alignment of new US Product MDM timelines with Project Diamond timelines will impact the overall Project Diamond schedule and will be assessed via change control process.

In case of delay with the new US Product MDM or the timelines do not sync up with Project Diamond, Project Diamond will interface with existing DSS based Product master.

A decision of the same will be collectively made between Sanofi Digital and ZS and no later than the Design phase.

In this case, Project Diamond will be designed to be scalable and flexible to simplify integration with new US Product MDM at a later stage which will be done through change control.

The scope of the SOW includes migration of DSS and SGDW to Cloud.

All applicable modules such as Business Rules Engine (BRE), Data Quality Manager (DQM) and Orchestration, will be set-up common for both DSS and SGDW

In case of any enhancements done to DSS / SGDW post initiation of Project Diamond, Sanofi Digital will track and manage them.

ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to determine if a change request is required to reapply these changes in the cloud environment.

Any minor enhancements such as bug fixes, minor critical business requirements (less than 80 hours), up to a reasonable limit and without extending the project timeline, will be covered as part of the project.

Any major changes such as onboarding a new data source or addition of a new BU etc.

will go through change control process

Activities that need design, code or testing changes that are not included as part of current scope, will be considered as enhancements.

If the requested changes/enhancements are beyond the original scope of the project, and/or exceed the overall timeline of the project, a separate change request will be raised with additional cost to extend the project scope/timeline

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Roles & Responsibilities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Roles & Responsibilities

ZS understands that the success of this project is driven by right mix of people with technology and domain expertise.

ZS is committed to deploy a technical team well-versed in the technologies such as AWS, Snowflake and Informatica to ensure success for Project Diamond.

If at any time, the services provided by a ZS resource is not in accordance with Sanofi’s expectations, ZS will have maximum of 2 weeks to correct the matter.

In the even it is not resolved, Sanofi has the right to request a replacement and ZS will take immediate action.

ZS will notify Sanofi of any lead time required to replace the resource for full transparency.

To

ensure

that

Project

Diamond

design

and

architecture

are

being

deployed

as

per

best

practices,

ZS

will

engage

with

its

AWS,

Snowflake

and

Informatica

partners

as

necessary

and

at

various

points

so

that

Sanofi

can

get

maximum

benefits

out

of

the

technical

tools.

Following

is

a

description

of

high-level

roles

and

responsibilities

of

key

ZS

and

Sanofi

Resources:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: ZS roles & responsibilities:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Onshore ZS Team, Onshore ZS Team
Row 2: Program Sponsor, Strategic advisor to the project team
Ensures the highest quality client service across all projects and work streams
Row 3: Engagement Manager, Overall engagement relationships accountability
Responsible for overall delivery and client satisfaction
Ensures that expertise is always available to the client by engaging the right ZS experts for each project task
Row 4: Solution / Technical Architect, Expertise in AWS/ Informatica Cloud/ Snowflake and Lead by implementing best practices in these technologies
Review the ongoing implementation solution for improvement
opportunities
Lead feasibility analysis, select the technologies that provide the
best solution and identify the products available that will best fit
the solution proposed
Row 5: Senior Business Analyst/Integration Lead, Has good domain knowledge around Sanofi data and business applications
Engage and communicate with Sanofi stakeholders as required during product requirements definition, design and development
Coordination with onshore team on data findings and data analysis results
Row 6: Project/Testing coordinator, Accountability for communication and management of status, risks, and issues
Conduct status meetings; manage product backlog and track progress
Row 7: Offshore ZS Team, Offshore ZS Team
Row 8: Offshore Sanofi Delivery Lead, Accountable for the delivery of the all the Sanofi projects from India
Ensures that all the Sanofi workstreams are well connected and share learnings
Row 9: Technical Team Lead, Responsible for day-to-day management of project
Responsible to maintaining timelines and quality of all deliverables
Provide oversight of technical solution design and implementation
Ensure adherence to Sanofi program management framework and standards in delivery
Row 10: Sr. Developer, Review requirements and design and raise clarifying questions before starting build

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0062
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: ZS roles & responsibilities:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Review design and develop POCs along with the design team
Review and contribute to the design with the overall solution in mind
Lead and perform code reviews, with the understanding of overall solution and client coding standards
Lead with the Design/build activities
Manage day to day build activities and coordinate with offshore delivery lead to ensure progress is being made as per plan
Row 11: Developer(s), Work on building application based on the approved requirements and design
Reconfigure Business Rules which are required
Establish Data Model /design and development guidelines and best practices
Develop code and perform unit testing
Row 12: Solution/Technical Architect, Provide guidance to the team in designing the solution taking advantage of the existing assets maintaining a balance between architecture requirements and specific client need
Expertise in AWS/ Informatica Cloud/ Snowflake and Lead by implementing best practices in these technologies
Row 13: Test Lead, Create test scenarios for system integration testing and map them to requirements in RTM
Conducts periodic reviews of test cases prepared by associates to ensure positive and negative scenarios are covered and track progress
Ensures UAT defects are tested first in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest
Conduct RCA for cancelled and missed defects and implement corrective actions as per discussion with test lead /manager
Row 14: Test Analyst (s), Prepare test cases based on scenarios defined
For an issues / concerns around design, requirement and scenarios – raise the same in defect tracker and discuss the same on daily calls
Create test status report tool and share daily status report using the tool
During UAT phase, retest UAT defects in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest
Row 15: AWS, Snowflake, IICS Professional Services, AWS, Snowflake, IICS Professional Services

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0063
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: ZS roles & responsibilities:
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 16: Snowflake Solutions Architect, Detailed architecture review and support during Design as per Snowflake best practices
Advise on design best practices for integrating with Informatica Cloud
Advise on Integration with interfacing Snowflake based platforms like IQVIA, Reltio
Priority support to debug any show stopper issues in Snowflake
Resolve any performance issues during test cycles
Support for Historical data migration from legacy platform for incremental data sources (where source does not provide full history)
Immediate priority support for Snowflake issues during production runs
Row 17: Informatica Solutions Architect, Detailed architecture review and support during Design as per best practices for IICS and integration with Snowflake
Guidance and review for IICS design and share best practices to make scalable process
Row 18: Informatica Solutions Delivery Consultant, Priority support to debug any show stopper issues related to IICS or IICS-Snowflake integration
Resolve any performance issues during test cycles
Support any code migration from Informatica on-prem to IICS (if required)
Priority support for IICS issues during production runs
Row 19: AWS Solutions Architect, Detailed architecture review and support during Design as per AWS best practices
Advise on design best practices for hosting Snowflake and Informatica platforms

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Sanofi roles & responsibilities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Program Manager, Act at the liaison between ZS and Sanofi leadership
Row 2: PMO, Work in tandem with ZS Project/Testing Coordinator to collaborate with various Sanofi core team/downstream/Infrastructure teams
Provide status updates to Sanofi core team
Row 3: System Owner, Overall business requirement/demand owner
Help with DSS / SGDW retirement plan from a Sanofi standpoint
Row 4: User Project Leader (UPL), Define & Approve user requirements
Provide user requirements for processes where documentation is not available
Provide inputs to come up with effective system retirement and data migration plan
Row 5: Technical Architect, Review & Approve design documents and architecture
Row 6: IS Project Leader (IPL), Review & Approve design documents and architecture
Approve code migration and deployments
Review & Approve test strategy/test cases/test results
Row 7: Validation Expert (VEX), Help define validation plan and PUMA deliverables for the project
Provide inputs to define test strategy, retirement plan and data migration plan
Row 8: Business Quality Representative, Provide business inputs to formulate test strategy

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Risk Management

Detailed

RAID

(Risk,

Action,

Issue,

Decision)

log

process

establishment

during

the

project

planning

and

initiation

phase

will

help

in

early

identification

of

risks

and

issues

and

thus

taking

timely

actions

and

decisions

accordingly.

Following

are

some

key

risks

and

their

mitigation

approach:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0067
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_path: C:\Users\HP\vs_studio_projects\pharma_copilot\data\sample_docs\ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Risk Management
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach
Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown
requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with Sanofi Data Management team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control
Row 2: Onboarding, Delay in Onboarding of ZS resources to Sanofi network and Project Diamond tool stack, Sanofi Digital to help initiate onboarding of ZS resources prior to initiation of the project to avoid any delays to Project Timelines
Row 3: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs, Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions
Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues
Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control
Row 4: Infrastructure, Unavailability of Sanofi GIS for code migration across environments or resolving infrastructure issues, Sanofi Digital /GIS will identify appropriate backup resources to resolve infrastructure issues as well as expedite code/data migration requests
Row 5: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control
Row 6: Transition, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner and onboard them prior to the first release of BU (Immunology) into Project Diamond

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.chunk_id: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx::chunk_0068
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.source_file: ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS  SGDW Migration to Cloud_Including Technical Partners v1 11.05.2021_v1.0.docx

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Risk Management
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach
Row 7: Product MDM, Syncing new US Commercial Product MDM design, testing, go-live timelines with Project Diamond, Finalize timelines for syncing each release of Project Diamond with Product MDM during requirements phase. Close co-ordination is required between Project Diamond and US Product MDM team at the start of every release to re-assess the syncing of testing, go-live timelines. Any changes required due to the delay in planned timelines will be assessed via change control

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: Budget
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Budget

*

As

the

solution

will

be

hosted

in

Sanofi’s

cloud

environment,

there

is

no

cost

assumed

for

infrastructure

expenses

or

license

fees

The total maximum amount payable under this IPA will not exceed $ 1,592,895.

The not-to-exceed value quoted above is an estimate of the cost of the anticipated Services to be rendered and is not a guaranteed fee.

The total compensation paid to Vendor will be based on actual Services provided in accordance with the MSA.

The project plan and pricing set forth in this Sanofi project diamond DSS & SGDW Migration do not take into consideration any price escalations, delays, shortages, and other disruptions that may occur due to the global COVID-19 (also known as coronavirus) pandemic and actions taken in response to the pandemic by businesses, local, state, and national authorities, or by ZS in its reasonable discretion (collectively, a “COVID-19 Event).

Sanofi acknowledges and agrees that ZS is permitted to adjust the timelines, fees and/or personnel assignments set forth in this SOW if, in its reasonable judgement, ZS determines that a Covid-19 Event will prevent it from delivering the project as set forth in this SOW.

ZS will provide Sanofi with prompt written notice of any such determination and, upon Sanofi’s request, will execute a change order to this SOW documenting any such changes.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have indicated their acceptance of the terms of this IPA by the signatures set forth below on the dates indicated.

Each individual signing for a corporate entity hereby personally warrants his or her legal authority to bind that entity.

Accepted

and

Agreed

to:

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: GLOSSARY
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Term, Description
Row 1: API, Application Program Interface
Row 2: AWS, Amazon Web services
Row 3: BAU, Business as usual
Row 4: BIA, Business Intelligence & Analytics; refers to DW&BI applications in Sanofi ecosystem
Row 5: BRE, Business Rule Engine
Row 6: BU, Business Unit
Row 7: CARS, Contract Administration and Reporting System
Row 8: CIM, Customer Information Management; refers to the system with SAS datasets that provide analytics to downstream commercial operation applications
Row 9: DDD, Drug Distribution Data. Sales data of direct and indirect sales at the zip code level (retail) or at the outlet level (non-retail/hospital) every month.
Row 10: DQF, Data Quality Framework
Row 11: DSS, Decision Support System. The module of Quantum that deals with sales and marketing information and the analysis of that information to assist in creating forecasts.
Row 12: ETL, Extract, Transform, Load
Row 13: DW, Data Warehouse
Row 14: FO, Field Operations
Row 15: ITS, IT Services
Row 16: IICS, Informatica cloud services
Row 17: IMM, Immunology
Row 18: IQVIA, Provider of Customer & Sales data
Row 19: Integration Hub, The central component connecting DSS and the various customer data consumers. It also stages the raw files from the various source systems.
Row 20: Liquid Hub, Specialty Pharmacy Data Aggregator and Source Provider
Row 21: MS, Multiple Sclerosis
Row 22: ONC, Oncology
Row 23: PDB, Patient Database
Row 24: PMO, Project Management Office
Row 25: RBD, Rare blood disorder
Row 26: RCM, Regional Customer Master
Row 27: RD, Rare disease
Row 28: S3, Simple storage services

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.heading: GLOSSARY
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - DSS SGDW Mig.text:
Columns: Term, Description
Row 29: SAS, Statistical Analysis System is a software suite developed by the SAS Institute for advanced analytics, multivariate analyses, business intelligence, data management, and predictive analytics.
Row 30: SFTP, Stands for “Secure File Transfer Protocol” , is a network protocol that provides file transfer and manipulation functionality over any reliable data stream
Row 31: SGDW, Sanofi Genzyme Data Warehouse
Row 32: TRACE, Refers to the system/application that handles promotional activities/events for Sanofi employees and CSO’s.
Row 33: UI, User Interface
Row 34: Veeva CRM, Veeva CRM is a platform used for customer management and helps to transform customer engagement
Row 35: VEX, Sanofi Validation Experts

Vendor Project Contact: Gagan Bhatara

The following terms and conditions apply solely to work performed under this IPA and do not apply to any other IPA.

The IPA is governed by the terms of the MSA dated February 22, 2021 between and ZS Associates, Inc.

(“MSA”).

To the extent there is any conflict between the terms and conditions of this IPA and the MSA, the MSA controls.

All capitalized terms in this IPA will have the same defined meanings as set forth in the MSA.

Exit Clause: With respect to the Services outlined in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience with immediate effect.

In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to termination.

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Project Summary
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text:
Project Summary

As

part

of

the

ongoing

project

diamond

releases

planned

for

this

year,

there

is

a

need

from

the

OneCI

team

to

update

the

existing

OneCI

Global

views

to

include

the

new

and

missing

fields

and

update

business

rules

as

per

the

documentation

shared

by

OneCI/LUCI

Team.

Additionally, there is an assumed total capacity of 90 hours: on top of the scoped list of enhancements mentioned in Table -1 under the scope section.

Any additional enhancements on top of the scope will be added to the backlog, prioritized (with the product owners) and implemented within the allocated 90 hours capacity (to be planned during of sprint-3 that is inclusive of development, testing, UAT and deployment activities over a period of 3 weeks).

The type of enhancements assumed in sprint-3 are minor attribute-level or business logic updates, no new data ingestion or major architectural changes are assumed to be in scope.

The

overall

timeline

of

this

scope

of

work

is

10

weeks

–

From

22nd

July

2024

through

27th

September

2024

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: In Scope
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text:
Columns: Enhancement Subject Area, Enhancement description, Sprint
Row 1: Territory, Get territory hierarchy from Valign instead of CRM, 2
Row 2: Event, Updates in the format attribute, 1
Row 3: Interactions, Implementation of submitted interactions logic, 1
Row 4: Interactions, Integration of employee information in RTE data, 1
Row 5: Field Days, Remapping of Time of Territory values based on US CRM, 1
Row 6: Field Days, Correcting calendar and calling days logic, 1
Row 7: OCCP, Updates in the is_occp flag based on values flowing in US CRM, 1
Row 8: , Employee, same employee for all lines, 1
Row 9: Coaching, Coaching report data filtering on Survey Type to avoid duplicates, 2
Row 10: RTE, Updates in the first/last emails logics, 1
Row 11: Product, Updates in logic for disease code, 1
Row 12: CLM, Changes in CLM Interactions logics, 1
Row 13: New attributes addition, Integration of 10 new fields based on new business requirements, 2
Row 14: New attributes addition, Incorporation of 53 new timestamp attributes across 26 views, 2

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Roles & Responsibilities
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text:
Columns: Role & Team, Responsibilities
Row 1: Onshore ZS Team, Onshore ZS Team
Row 2: Senior Business Analyst/Integration Lead, Has good domain knowledge around Sanofi data and business applications.
Engage and communicate with Sanofi stakeholders as required during product requirements definition, design, and development.
Coordination with onshore team on data findings and data analysis results
Row 3: Offshore ZS Team, Offshore ZS Team
Row 4: Technical Team Lead, Responsible for day-to-day management of project
Responsible to maintaining timelines and quality of all deliverables.
Provide oversight of technical solution design and implementation
Ensure adherence to Sanofi program management framework and standards in delivery.
Row 5: Sr. Developer, Review requirements and design and raise clarifying questions before starting build.
Review design and develop POCs along with the design team.
Review and contribute to the design with the overall solution in mind.
Lead and perform code reviews, with the understanding of overall solution and client coding standards.
Lead with the Design/build activities.
Manage day to day build activities and coordinate with offshore delivery lead to ensure progress is being made as per plan.
Row 6: Test Analyst, Prepare test cases based on scenarios defined
For an issues / concerns around design, requirement and scenarios – raise the same in defect tracker and discuss the same on daily calls 
Create test status report tool and share daily status report using the tool
During UAT phase, retest UAT defects in SIT before they are delivered to UAT for retest

ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.heading: Risk Management
ZS SOW - Project Diamond - OneCI enhance.text:
Columns: Area, Risks, Mitigation Approach
Row 1: Requirements, Managing unknown
requirements / processes, ZS will closely coordinate with OneCI team to help in proactively pre-empting any issues related to missed requirements and any unknown requirements uncovered after requirements phase and that impacts project timelines will be assessed through Change Control
Row 2: Change Requests, Changes to scope or delivery schedule, delay project delivery and increase project management costs., Assign a single point of contact from Sanofi Digital as the product owner who is accountable for overall project plan and liaisons with other Sanofi functions.
Weekly status updates meetings with the core team to highlight potential issues early to minimize impact on timeline. Periodic Steering Committee meetings to highlight / escalate issues.
Any changes to scope will be assessed through Change Control
Row 3: Delivery, Unavailability of downstream teams for testing, Both ZS and downstream teams to get aligned on the timeline and agree on the complete set of integration test scenarios during requirements phase, any deviations from the agreed upon timeline to be managed through Change Control
Row 4: OneCI/LUCI team’s availability, Timely onboarding of managed services partner and their availability, Sanofi Digital to plan production operations team in a time bound manner

The project plan and pricing set forth in this Sanofi project diamond OneCI enhancements do not take into consideration any price escalations, delays, shortages, and other disruptions that may occur due to the global COVID-19 (also known as coronavirus) pandemic and actions taken in response to the pandemic by businesses, local, state, and national authorities, or by ZS in its reasonable discretion (collectively, a “COVID-19 Event).

Sanofi acknowledges and agrees that ZS is permitted to adjust the timelines, fees and/or personnel assignments set forth in this SOW if, in its reasonable judgement, ZS determines that a Covid-19 Event will prevent it from delivering the project as set forth in this SOW.

ZS will provide Sanofi with prompt written notice of any such determination and, upon Sanofi’s request, will execute a change order to this SOW documenting any such changes.

1. Project Summary:

This project involves collaboration with Sanofi’s PSS Analytics and Reporting team to

enhance existing dashboards and develop new dashboards, including end-to-end

support for User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and launch. The scope includes

development and launch of the Dupixent PSS Performance Dashboard, enhancements

to the Dupixent Patient Onboarding Dashboard and Dupixent PSS Executive

Dashboard, and enhancement and launch support for the Dupixent PSS On-Therapy

Dashboard. Based on the requirements, the project is targeted commence on

1/19/2026 and end on 5/22/2026

Services under this IPA

ZS SoW Dupixent PSS 2026 Q1 Enhancements.text:
2.3 Assumptions

• For the engagement, the ZS team will be utilizing the PV file, dispense file, PSS View file

and other required datasets to develop data views and reports

• The data provided by upstream will be quality checked by the upstream teams and will

ensure the quality of the same

• Delays arising from data availability or other dependencies may affect the timelines of

individual deliverables, potentially impacting the overall project schedule and the

associated professional fees.

• The Sanofi PSS A&R team will be responsible for finalizing business rules

• Sanofi will ensure the availability of relevant stakeholders to share business

requirements, rules, and other project-related guidance.

• The project timeline assumes that all dependencies and prerequisites are met as

scheduled

• The entire ZS team (except the UI/UX SME) will be based out of ZS India offices

• India ZS team will overlap with the Sanofi stakeholders during US ET AM hours

• ZS resources will be onboarded to the Sanofi network prior to the initiation of the project.

Any delay may impact on the project’s start

• ZS will get the required support from Sanofi to address and open queries and respond to

information required by ZS teams

• Any reduction in hours owing to Offshore holidays will not be carried forward to the

following weeks and will account for reduction in the week of occurrence

• Current scope and budget estimations include activities defined under the section 2.1

2.4 Timelines

• The work is proposed to commence on 1/19/2026 and end on 5/22/2026. The

proposed plan is as below:

• The enhancements will be bundled in 4-week releases, ensuring incremental

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Exit Clause:
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text:
Exit Clause:

With respect to the Services outlines in this SOW only, Sanofi may terminate this SOW, in whole or in part, for convenience to terminating immediately, providing 10 days’ notice.

In the event this SOW is terminated for any reason, ZS shall cease the performance of the Services on the date of such termination, and Sanofi shall be responsible only for the payment of fees for Services performed by ZS up to the termination date.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Scope Activities and Deliverables
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text:
Scope Activities and Deliverables

Major defects: Major defects are those which substantially prevent the deliverable from functioning or performing in accordance with the acceptance criteria set forth herein for such deliverable.

Major defects materially impact Client’s ability to use the deliverable and/or cause issues with the trust and reliability of the deliverable and its output.

Critical defects: Critical defects are the most serious type of defects.

Critical defects render the deliverable unusable in accordance with the acceptance criteria set forth herein for such deliverable and/or could cause harm to Client.

Critical defects would put Client at serious risk of liability issues, lawsuits and compliance issues.

The

start

and

end

dates

specified

above

might

change

as

per

ongoing

discussions

between

ZS

and

Sanofi

and

they

will

be

formalized

through

a

change

order.

ZS will provide hypercare support for 2 weeks and shadow two full data refreshes as per source data frequency after the Go Live of every release or until all Critical or Major defects reported within this hypercare window have been resolved.

The above schedule of the delivery milestones is for the complete duration of Change Order, which may have to be revisited based on the actual scenarios and any dependencies.

In such a scenario both the parties will agree on the revised plan through change control process.

In case any delay occurs due to reasons not related to ZS and impacts the deliverable milestone, the milestone payment schedule will be managed through change control process with alignment from Sanofi and ZS leadership.

Row 1: Planning & Architecture, Work with Sanofi stakeholders on detailed planning for requirements workshops and Project Timelines
Creation of Detailed Project Plan Incorporating Foundation, Core and Analytics Activities
Identifying Key Stakeholders
Aligning of Project Deliverables (Plan and RACI) and Project Architecture
Identifying Dependency with Omni Channel Project
Work with Sanofi stakeholders to define data loading plan and process for sources that directly provide data to CIM Diamond Analytics.

High level and Low-level project plan

RACI for project activities

Change Management/Communication Plan, Project plan reviewed & sign-off by PO and Program Manager
Architecture and Technology Finalization by Sanofi Architects
Row 2: Requirements, Setting up Requirement Workshop for Foundation, Core (i.e., Frameworks DQ, Audit, Time Travel, TDQF, Data Sharing and BRE) and Analytics
Engage boundary systems and stakeholders to coordinate system changes.
Engage with Sanofi Clients and Data vendors for Data Exchange Mechanism
Document user requirements, Functional requirements, and process flows., List of detailed Requirement categories for each area or TA. Be able to calculate much has been completed.
Outbound and boundary systems identified, work with the respective boundary systems for integration with Diamond Plus – Analytics
Test Strategy

Create Architecture Specification and Solution Design documents for sign off.

Data Exchange mechanism

Framework integrations

Row 4: Build, Build ingestions, audit, DQ, PSA, DWH, DMT, BRE, Functional Layer and extracts based on requirements.
Implement robust error handling, audit, and DQ for E2E process from ingestion to consumption data set.
Code per low level design and execute unit testing
Transformation and Business Rule configurations for Foundation, Core and Analytics
Configuration of datasets into technical frameworks
End to end automation and orchestration configuration, Complete build per requirements.
Unit Test documents
E2E Code review with Sanofi Data Analyst and Data Engineers
Demo of frameworks and processes, Review code with Sanofi COE
Build closure sign off from data analyst
Row 5: SIT, Create Test Strategy, Traceability Matrix, SIT scripts, and test report.
Preparation / Execution of test cases considering positive and negative data scenarios for each release as per rationalization & design.
Reconciliation testing including observation discussion with the boundary system team
End to end testing of all modules built as part of each release.
Document SIT results, defects, and co-ordinate with development team for defect resolution.
Orchestration testing with dry run of end-to-end automated jobs within the application., Test Strategy
Test Plan document
Test Case scripts
Test scenarios
Test case scripts
RTM Matrix

Review with Sanofi business owners

Prepare UAT Test Cases/Scripts.
Collaborate with Sanofi BOS users and support them perform UAT validations.
Defect Management – Defect Triaging and resolution
Support User Training to end users (In and Outbound consumers)
Confirm both technical and user readiness for go-live., UAT Test Plan document
UAT Test Case preparation
UAT Test scenarios
UAT case scripts
UAT test execution report and result, UAT exit criteria: No critical or major defects.
UAT Users and Sanofi Digital Team provide UAT signoff

Create Release plan with list of all components to be migrated to production.
Create and route all required documentation.
Deploy all code & enable required settings.
Perform hyper-care support activities, including monitor processes, provide support if needed, bug resolution and triage. Perform KT with AIMS teams and create any KT Articles and SOPs required.
Conduct workshops for knowledge transfer to concerned stakeholders identified by Sanofi, Release Plan for Sanofi Quality team and technical deployment (with code component list for each release)
Release Report Authorization for Use
SOPs
Operations Checklist, Hypercare exit criteria: No critical or major defects.
Signoff from business owners
Sign off from Sanofi Diamond leadership.

Row 8: Change Management, Communication and Collaboration – Collaborate with Sanofi Digital and BOS to identify key stakeholders across TAs and Business Units to engage with them during entire lifecycle of each release.
Train users on the DMT Access, the Analytics Access, IDE and No-Code Tools, DMT data sets, Functional Layer Data Sets, Audit and DQ Dashboard. Schedule ongoing office hours for users until Hypercare period of the release Define change management, training, communication channels and communication strategy, & plan for all downstream applications
Capture feedbacks during the trainings and update as needed.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Diamond Analytics Roadmap
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text:
Diamond Analytics Roadmap

Changes to the project plan after finalization during the Planning phase will be reviewed with Sanofi and ZS leadership to evaluate impact on the project timelines, effort, and cost.

ZS will provide the high-level estimate (effort, cost and time) in agreed upon number of days to Sanofi.

Existing

operations

teams

for

CIM

will

be

available

during

Project’s

implementation

to

clarify

any

questions

on

the

processes

or

data

in

existing

platforms.

It is assumed that current DSS / SGDW / CIM business rules and data layouts are readily available to facilitate an efficient requirement gathering phase.

For processes where there is no documentation available, ZS will collaborate with Sanofi to document the process during requirements phase.

Given that Diamond Omni Channel migration will be a parallel program, ZS Diamond team will work collaboratively with the Omni team and ensure Diamond is integrated with the new Omni Channel platform as per the finalized Sanofi architecture and strategy.

Also, Diamond team will keep its project plan aligned with the Omni Channel project plan to ensure seamless integrations.

ZS Diamond will support integration and testing with scope-defined dependent systems while Diamond team will be responsible for testing the Diamond side of the integration, each of those dependent system teams will be responsible for performing testing and any integration required within their system.

Diamond will provide these systems with integration specifications and support E2E testing with data exchanges & corresponding validation.

Also, if testing support is required beyond Diamond Analytics hyper care period, it will be evaluated / estimated separately depending on the nature of the required support.

This engagement is based on a fixed effort and fixed price for the given scope and duration of the engagement.

ZS onshore and offshore resource holidays/leave policy will be in accordance with the resource location and ZS policy.

2023 holiday schedule for ZS US & India offices mentioned in Appendix A.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Budget
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text:
Budget

The price and invoicing schedule for this Change Order is defined in the Scope Activities and Deliverables section.

Fees to be invoiced on a Fixed Price basis, as per the above table that represents the invoice schedule that are tied to the delivery milestone.

ZS will invoice Sanofi in accordance with the Invoice Dates set forth in Section 2.

The total Project fees authorized under this CO shall not exceed the amount of USD $3,992,895 4,787,895 ($2,400,000 Diamond Analytics (to be invoiced) + $846,497 Remaining Diamond Phase 1 (to be invoiced) + $746,398 (invoiced for the completed Diamond Phase 1 releases) +$795,000 Diamond Analytics Replanning Estimates (to be invoiced) without the prior written consent of Sanofi and supported by a mutually executed change request.

All amounts quoted in this IPA are exclusive of any taxes, expenses, etc.

All amounts mentioned in this IPA are exclusive of taxes and the same shall be invoiced at actuals as and when applicable.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Service Level Agreement – Timeliness
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text:
Service Level Agreement – Timeliness

The schedule variation % of time measures for this penalty shall be determined on the ratio of the difference between the actual start and end date to the difference between the planned start date and end date.

The Schedule Variation % will be calculated basis of the delayed delivery milestone.

ZS shall complete the milestones within a schedule variation of 100% of the estimated schedule (unless Sanofi and ZS mutually agree on any deviations from the schedule).

In the event the schedule variation exceeds 100%, ZS shall be liable to pay service credit.

The service credit shall apply 1% each week of the delayed delivery milestone.

In no event shall the total amount of service level credits shall exceed, 12% of the delivery milestone (the “Total Service Credit Cap”).

The service credit shall only be applicable if the delay in the timeline is solely due to the fault of ZS, and such credit shall be the sole and exclusive remedy to Sanofi.

The applicability of service credit will be discussed regularly in the operational committee between Sanofi and ZS.

ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.heading: Accommodating Sanofi Internalization:
ZSA_Sanofi_SOW_CR.docx.text:
Accommodating Sanofi Internalization:

If during the program Sanofi internalizes resources (Data engineers, Data Analyst, etc.), the Parties will renegotiate workload, RACI & Cost impact in good faith.

The change will be applied on release level and Sanofi will provide 10 business days’ notice to ZS for resource roll-off.

All such changes will be documented in a separate change order.

IN

WITNESS

WHEREOF,

the

Parties

have

executed

this

Change

Order

effective

as

of

the

Effective

Date

set

forth

above.
